changedFiles.reason: Added the approved platform-neutral candidate verification design, native platform adapters, GitLab Free atomic compatibility gate, evidence-strength model, failure semantics, and future test strategy.
Created the Work Item Contract skeleton.
Recorded the design handoff, user corrections, review findings, residual risks, and verification evidence.
Generated Cockpit status for the active documentation Work Item.

sourcesUsed: GitHub documentation for Merge Queue and merge_group events
GitLab documentation for merge request pipelines, Merge Trains, CI variables, resource groups, protected branches, and merge request APIs

scenarioCoverage.scenario: GitHub Merge Queue and GitLab Merge Trains use platform-generated candidate commits while sharing one Core verification contract.
evidence: Core Verification Contract and Native Platform Adapters sections in the design document
Independent documentation accuracy, completeness, and consistency review

scenarioCoverage.scenario: GitLab Free receives equivalent candidate verification through an isolated native-Git merge and a serialized final merge gate.
evidence: GitLab Free Compatibility Gate, Failure Semantics, and Security and Trust Boundaries sections
Design correction from deferred Merge API action to an atomic fast-forward Git ref update

scenarioCoverage.scenario: The design does not turn Cockpit Core into a queue, branch graph, or platform workflow engine.
evidence: Decisions, Non-Goals, Core Verification Contract, and Responsibility Boundaries sections

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Distinguish current implemented behavior from proposed future behavior and do not claim that the design is already shipped.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The Status and Non-Goals sections explicitly identify the document as an approved future design and defer runtime implementation.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Keep Cockpit Core platform-neutral; platform event parsing, queue semantics, credentials, and merge execution belong to adapters.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The Core Verification Contract accepts only candidate/base facts, while Responsibility Boundaries and adapter sections retain platform behavior outside Core.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Prefer native platform candidate generation, provide a safe native-Git fallback where required, and fail closed when candidate freshness cannot be established.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The capability matrix, GitLab Free compatibility flow, evidence-strength table, and failure semantics define native preference, atomic fallback, freshness validation, and fail-closed downgrade behavior.

risk.detail: Documentation-only change. Runtime merge behavior is unchanged; future implementation must independently verify platform edition, permissions, credentials, protected-branch enforcement, and atomic update behavior.

observedIssues: The initial draft used the GitLab Merge API after verification, but review found that its source-only SHA guard and deferred pipeline timing did not bind the final target tree; the design now uses an atomic normal fast-forward Git push.

The initial draft overstated bot-only enforcement and project access token availability on GitLab Free; the design now uses a Free-compatible automation credential and treats privileged human bypass as an external trust boundary.

Accuracy review found that an unprotected merge-request pipeline cannot safely consume protected automation credentials; the final gate now runs in a protected target-branch-controlled coordinator pipeline.
The initial draft did not distinguish candidate construction from final freshness for native platforms; a capability matrix and generic integration contract were added.

residualRisks.detail: GitLab editions and self-managed configurations expose different credential and protected-branch capabilities. Later implementation must validate actual instance capabilities and must not infer governed-path safety from hostname or edition labels alone.

intentAlignment.rationaleValidated: The design gives parallel teams one platform-neutral verification contract, uses native queues where available, provides a Git-based GitLab Free compatibility gate, and keeps queue state outside AI Cockpit Core.

reviewReadiness.reason: All required verification passed; residual risk remains documented.
expectedReviewFocus: Confirm the atomic fast-forward push and freshness checks close the governed GitLab Free merge race without claiming privileged bypass is impossible.
Confirm native GitHub and GitLab modes distinguish candidate construction from final freshness.
Confirm Contract, Summary, Archive, and AI Cockpit Core remain platform-neutral and no runtime capability is claimed as implemented.

userCorrectionsCaptured: Aggregation is Git's responsibility; AI Cockpit should not implement Git aggregation or queue semantics again.
GitLab Free must receive a native-Git compatibility fallback; architectural boundary purity is not the HCI North Star.
userCorrectionSolidification: The Contract intent and acceptance criteria bind queue ownership to Git and hosting platforms while limiting AI Cockpit Core to candidate/base verification.
The design's North Star and HCI Constraint plus GitLab Free Compatibility Gate require adapters to absorb safe platform capability gaps and define the atomic fallback path.

checkpointEvidence.detail: Reviewed the final documentation-only scope, HCI constraints, platform sources, acceptance conditions, scenario coverage, and out-of-scope runtime files before editing the design.
Reviewed the completed design, five-strategy documentation review corrections, final scope, acceptance mapping, user-correction solidification, and residual implementation risks before finish.

knownGaps: No runtime adapter, installer profile, CI configuration, protected-branch automation, or merge credential setup is implemented by this Work Item.
The future implementation must test GitLab.com Free and representative Self-Managed Free configurations separately.
overclaimPrevention: Do not report completion for checks or behavior that were not verified.
